name: Sugar (Sucrose)
description: Sucrose disaccharide (glucose+fructose). Refined from cane or beet.

name: High Fructose Corn Syrup
common_use: Cheap liquid sweetener typically 55% fructose 45% glucose.
description: Industrial corn-derived sweetener; cheaper than sucrose.

name: Honey
common_use: Natural sweetener; primarily glucose+fructose plus trace enzymes.
description: Bee-produced. Botulism risk in infants under 12 months.

name: Salt (Sodium Chloride)
description: Mineral salt NaCl. Toxic to most species in excess (cats especially sensitive).

name: Wheat Flour (unbleached)
description: Ground wheat endosperm. Contains gluten.

name: Wheat Flour (bleached)
common_use: Same as unbleached but chemically whitened.
description: Bleached with chlorine gas or benzoyl peroxide.

name: Corn Starch
description: Pure starch from corn endosperm.

name: Palm and Canola Oil Blend
description: Palm oil + canola oil. Sustainability concerns with palm.

name: Butter
description: Dairy product. Contains casein and lactose.

name: Soy Lecithin
description: Phospholipid extracted from soybeans.

name: Vanillin (artificial)
description: Single compound; cheaper alternative to natural vanilla extract.

name: Natural Vanilla Extract
common_use: Aged ethanol extract of vanilla beans.
description: Contains vanillin plus 200+ trace compounds.

name: Cocoa (Dutch-processed)
common_use: Dark cocoa powder treated with alkali.
description: Contains theobromine — toxic to dogs and cats.

name: Chocolate
common_use: Mixture of cocoa solids cocoa butter sugar.
description: Contains theobromine. Darker = more toxic to pets.

name: Caffeine
description: Methylxanthine. Toxic to dogs and cats.

name: Caramel Color
common_use: Brown food coloring made by heating sugars.
description: Class IV caramel may contain 4-MEI which is a possible carcinogen.

name: Phosphoric Acid
description: Adds tartness. Linked to bone density loss in heavy consumers.

name: Citric Acid
description: Naturally in citrus; commercially produced via Aspergillus niger fermentation.

name: Carbon Dioxide (carbonation)
description: Pressurized gas dissolved in liquid.

name: Caffeine (natural)
description: Same compound as synthetic; same pet toxicity.

name: Natural Flavors
common_use: Proprietary plant-derived flavor blends; FDA permits this catch-all.
description: Composition usually undisclosed; allergen risk variable.

name: Artificial Flavors
description: Composition usually undisclosed.

name: Sodium Bicarbonate (Baking Soda)
description: NaHCO3. Releases CO2 when heated or with acid.

name: Baking Powder
description: Self-acting leavening.

name: Yeast
common_use: Saccharomyces cerevisiae ferments sugars to CO2 + ethanol.
description: Live fungus. Toxic to dogs and cats if eaten raw (rising dough = ethanol + GI rupture).

name: Xanthan Gum
description: Used as thickener in gluten-free baking.

name: Ascorbic Acid (Vitamin C)
description: Generally safe.

name: Sodium Benzoate
description: Safe at typical levels; can form benzene with vitamin C in heat/light.

name: Potassium Sorbate
description: Safe at typical levels.

name: Ethanol
common_use: Solvent and vehicle for vanilla extract etc.
description: Highly toxic to dogs cats and birds.

name: Xylitol
common_use: Sugar alcohol used as low-calorie sweetener.
description: EXTREMELY toxic to dogs (insulin response + hypoglycemia + liver failure). Lethal at 0.1g/kg.

name: Red 40 (Allura Red AC)
description: FDA approved; some countries restrict in foods marketed to children.

name: Yellow 5 (Tartrazine)
description: Some allergic reactions reported; banned in some EU contexts.
